best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the main realized edge: 30 trades, $2419 PnL, 349.6 PnL/exposure hour, 100% win rate, no slow >=12h trades. Worst MAE is material at -379.8 but not the global tail.
charm_wall_breakaway_long has only 5 trades but $986.6 PnL, 197.3 average PnL, 234.6 PnL/exposure hour, median MFE 146.2, no slow trades, and worst MAE -346.8.

breakout_long_1dte contributes $1311.2 over 20 VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades. Prior direct quality tightening hurt score and net profit, so preserve the baseline rather than tightening the same breakout gates again.
Extended-VWAP winners include 17 trades, $654.5 PnL, median MAE -14.45, median hold 1.18 minutes. Broad VWAP-extension pruning would likely destroy fast convex flow.

squeeze_long produced $721 over 10 trades with worst MAE -208.25 and no slow trades, but PnL/exposure is only 187.2 and median TP distance is very large, so it is useful but not the primary risk-reducer target.

broken_edges.evidence: 34 trades and $1261.5 PnL, but global worst MAE -513.4, 80-minute hold, delayed TP touch, and poor early progress. Static broad filters overlap protected extended-VWAP winners.
broken_edges.problem: Tail-heavy profitable profile; needs surgical risk discrimination rather than throughput pruning.

broken_edges.evidence: Prior 1DTE quality-gate runs reduced score by -355 to -594 and net profit by -317 to -558 while worsening worst MAE to -603.75.
broken_edges.problem: Direct breakout_long_1dte quality tightening is a known bad path.

broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE fallback has 5 trades and only $162.8 PnL. breakout_long_no_0dte stale subset has 2 trades, 40.5 PnL/exposure hour, median hold 52.6 minutes, and -250.25 worst MAE.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE stale fallback is low-efficiency exposure; useful to reduce tail/exposure only if VALID_0DTE and EXPIRED_0DTE behavior remain untouched.

broken_edges.evidence: No trades despite being active/enabled or router-listed in this sample; no evidence of reachable edge in the current regime.
broken_edges.problem: Dead or unreachable profiles should not consume optimizer budget in a risk-reducer pass.
broken_edges.profile: call_wall_acceptance_long, breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, trend_day_pullback_long, squeeze_long_no_0dte

market_regime_read: Short, high-win-rate, take-profit-only sample with 105 trades, 100% realized win rate, no slow >=12h trades, and low portfolio drawdown. The risk is hidden intratrade heat rather than realized loss. The current leader remains run_029; run_032 improved average MAE and exposure efficiency but lost $80 and 2 trades after the RSI-slope boundary probe, so the next risk-reducer should revert to surgical tail capture around known threshold misses, not broad filters or direct 1DTE tightening.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separates delayed charm-pin recoveries from fast extension winners.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Overfits a few delayed recoveries or blocks protected high-extension winners that resolve quickly.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path stats: first positive move, first +1.5/+2 points, MFE before MAE, first 15m MFE/MAE, TP touch timing, profile and VWAP-extension context.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long entries with high extension or recent-high-rejection that fail to make early positive progress after entry.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected fast extended-VWAP winners, total net profit >=98% of leader, and no slow trades.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Splits 1DTE pullback/reclaim behavior from breakout_long_1dte without damaging the proven fallback.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router overlap cannibalizes profitable breakout_long_1dte trades or the companion profile is unreachable.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Profile
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY profile routing, ES pullback/reclaim state, 1DTE option-map quality, profile-level MAE and PnL attribution.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1311, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -124.1, total net profit >=98% of leader, and total score flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: May route pin-risk tail states away from charm_pin_pullback_long and charm_wall_breakaway_long using an existing dormant concept.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant route is unreachable or cannibalizes clean charm-pin/charm-wall winners instead of isolating tail states.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin Avoid Dormant Activation Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router attribution, charm/pin state, profile standalone PnL, protected fast-winner overlap.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE high charm-wall or pin-risk state currently routed into charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated inactive profile has positive standalone PnL or reduces combined charm/pin worst MAE while total net profit remains >=98% of leader and protected fast-winner count is not materially reduced.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Captures recoverable pullback heat separately from deep traps.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Config-only proxy removes profitable weak-structure pullbacks; may need path-aware features to avoid suppressing the core PnL engine.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Companion Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: pullback_long entry state, early path behavior, weak structure distances, VWAP acceleration, delayed recovery markers.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE pullback_long entries with weak structure, low/negative VWAP acceleration, poor early MFE, and delayed recovery.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Retain >=90% pullback_long PnL while improving pullback worst MAE from -379.8 toward <= -300 and keeping total PnL/exposure flat or better.

next_experiments.config_bias: Use narrow boundary nudges around the already-enabled extended-VWAP rejection family, prioritizing the single failed boundary that caught the -513.4 tail before considering multi-boundary expansion. Avoid the prior RSI-slope widening path because run_032 lost score and net profit.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce global worst MAE without sacrificing the protected fast extended-VWAP winners.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The -513.4 charm-pin tail was a near miss on the extended-VWAP rejection veto by a small VWAP-distance boundary, while protected extended-VWAP winners are fast and low-MAE. A small non-RSI boundary expansion can remove the worst tail with less PnL loss than broad structure or setup filters.
next_experiments.name: Extended-VWAP Tail Boundary Re-Test
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE improvement with total net profit >=98.5% of run_029 and protected extended-VWAP winner retention >=95%.
next_experiments.risk: Boundary expansion can still catch clean extended winners, especially because extended-VWAP winners are a real fast edge.

next_experiments.config_bias: Tighten only the charm-pin tail-risk box around high pullback-from-high, high rolling-high distance, weak/negative VWAP acceleration, low ADX/RSI, and profile match. Do not add a broad setup-quality floor or global ES-structure threshold.
next_experiments.goal: Catch the worst charm-pin delayed recovery while preserving charm-pin throughput.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst trade had pullback-from-high 9.25, rolling-high distance 14.25, VWAP acceleration -0.0179, ADX 17.95, RSI 42.42, and delayed TP touch at 80 minutes. This is more specific than the profitable extended-VWAP population.
next_experiments.name: Charm Pin Tail Box Specificity Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE, charm-pin PnL retention >=90%, total score flat or better.
next_experiments.risk: Too much overlap with valid pullback/charm-pin winners because static pre-entry fields do not see delayed post-entry behavior.

next_experiments.config_bias: Isolate stale no-0DTE behavior only. Favor mild quality or exposure-efficiency trimming of breakout_long_no_0dte stale fallback; avoid touching VALID_0DTE routes or the single profitable EXPIRED_0DTE winner.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce inefficient stale fallback exposure and tail without damaging core valid-option flow.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE fallback contributes little PnL and breakout_long_no_0dte stale trades have poor efficiency and elevated MAE, so isolated trimming may improve risk-adjusted exposure quality.
next_experiments.name: Stale No-0DTE Fallback Efficiency Trim
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback PnL/exposure improvement, fallback worst MAE not worse, total net profit >=98.5% of leader, VALID_0DTE PnL unchanged.
next_experiments.risk: Very small sample; removing even low-efficiency winners can reduce score without visible drawdown benefit.

next_experiments.config_bias: Freeze breakout_long_1dte thresholds. If legal, test adding pullback_long_1dte as a companion one-dte router split with minimal local tuning and attribution-first evaluation.
next_experiments.goal: Improve 1DTE MAE composition without repeating failed direct 1DTE tightening.
next_experiments.hypothesis: VALID_1DTE_ONLY flow is profitable but average MAE is heavy. Prior direct tightening damaged score, so a companion split is more plausible than stricter breakout filters.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Companion Split Attribution Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1311, 1DTE average MAE improves, total score flat or better.
next_experiments.risk: Router ordering may cannibalize the proven breakout_long_1dte edge or produce no reachable trades.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep charm_wall_breakaway_long exits and take-profit behavior stable. Only test small admission attribution/holdout if it does not broaden weak wall-chase entries.
next_experiments.goal: Preserve the charm-wall convex edge while checking whether admission geometry remains robust.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Charm-wall breakaway is high-value but only 5 trades. Prior geometry admission improved objective, but follow-through was flat; this is a holdout/stability test, not an expansion priority.
next_experiments.name: Charm Wall Geometry Stability Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Profile PnL/exposure >=225, total worst MAE no worse than -513.4, total net profit >=98.5% of leader.
next_experiments.risk: Small sample makes false positives likely; loosening geometry can dilute a strong but sparse edge.

research_notes: Run_032 is a useful warning: loosening the extended-VWAP RSI-slope boundary improved exposure efficiency but reduced score and net profit, with no worst-MAE gain. The strongest remaining risk-reducer path is not broad pruning. Single-threshold candidates either retain only 79-84% PnL or still leave the -513.4 tail. The optimizer should prefer profile-aware surgical veto tuning and stale fallback isolation. Do not repeat direct 1DTE quality tightening, broad setup-quality floors, broad ES-VWAP max-distance caps, or pullback TP/VWAP expansion, all of which have prior negative evidence or obvious protected-winner overlap.
risk_controls: Require total net profit retention >=98.5% of run_029 for any risk-reducer promotion unless worst MAE improves materially below -403.
Require protected fast-winner retention checks, especially extended-VWAP winners, before promoting any entry veto or extension cap.
Reject experiments that reduce completed trades by more than 10% unless worst MAE improvement is large and profile PnL attribution supports it.
Keep slow >=12h trades at 0; any reintroduced slow hold is an automatic fail for this track.
Do not promote changes that only improve PnL/exposure by removing profitable trades while leaving worst MAE unchanged.
Validate profile-level MAE and PnL, not only aggregate score, because the aggregate 100% win-rate sample hides tail heat.
